data.ENCOUNTER: The sword levitates and flies at you. Roll a D6 and add your Shift. 7 or lower and lose 6 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is nothing but an empty, dusty hall. There are many footprints in the dust.
data.ENCOUNTER: You examine them closer and see that many imprints indicate running and some are hooves.

data.DESCRIPTION: Laying on the floor, crushed by a block of stone is an old warrior. His armour is buckled and rusted.
data.ENCOUNTER: You search them and find a broken item. Roll on WRT1, but something also triggers. Roll on L8TR.

data.DESCRIPTION: Behind some rubble you find a lost old shrine to Nevazator, carved with an old man holding a stick.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can reinvigorate this shrine with a correct offering and please the god, gaining 2 FP.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are stacks of rocks where which have fallen down from the walls. It's a cluttered, dirty space.
data.ENCOUNTER: You have a quick look but realise there is little to be seen. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is an old case in the middle of this empty room. It is very sturdy and made of heavy wood.
data.ENCOUNTER: You slide open the drawer and inside find a set of teeth and a set of fangs. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: Above you are many eyes carved into the ceiling. One in the centre is large and seems to swirl.
data.ENCOUNTER: Roll a D6 and add your Discipline. 7 or below you feel dizzy and collapse crushing 1 random potion.

data.DESCRIPTION: In the middle of this room is a large flickering illusion of an eyeball. It turns to you and blinks.
data.ENCOUNTER: It telepathically lifts a random scroll from your pack and casts it on you. It then disappears.

data.DESCRIPTION: The floor here is scratched with symbols and old books lay open. Candle sticks drip with wax.
data.ENCOUNTER: You search through and find a black feature, a vial of water and some dried fungi. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: A figure sits at a desk that is covered in paper. They look up and you see 1 eye. It is the CYCLOPS.
data.ENCOUNTER: You must fight them. If you survive, in the desk are two pouches. Roll on PT3 and PT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: Decorative arches span this space. On one is a light torch in a sconce. At the back is a basin.
data.ENCOUNTER: In the basin is a mound of stinking animal feed, which is inedible for humans. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: Standing on a plinth is a giant, empty stone urn. It has four monstrous heads carved into it.
data.ENCOUNTER: You recognise the Minotaur and the Cyclops but the other two are a mystery. Roll on L8TR.

data.DESCRIPTION: Beneath some cobwebs you find a tall, root wood Idol. You stand it up and see it is Radacina.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can reinvigorate this idol with a correct offering and please the god, gaining 2 FP.

data.DESCRIPTION: Dust has risen here to create clouds of white particles. You sense something is off though.
data.ENCOUNTER: You stumble through and realise you have triggered something. Roll on L8TR.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are four ornate statues here, one in each corner. They depict a man with a bull's head.
data.ENCOUNTER: One turns and throws a discus. Roll a D6 and add your Shift. 6 or below it hits you. Lose D6 +3 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is lined with fine columns. They are closely spaced to create a kind of miniature maze.
data.ENCOUNTER: Your path narrows as you see a spear shoot at you. Pass a PC10(1) to sidestep it or lose 8 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a large bull statue in the centre. It has a golden ring through its nose worth 4D6 GC.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you take it roll a D6. 3 or above the bull comes alive and attacks. Use EARTH CROC stats.

data.DESCRIPTION: In the middle of this shadowy, dust bowl of a room is a crouching figure. The MINOTAUR slowly rises.
data.ENCOUNTER: It charges you and attacks first. If you survive you find a chest at the rear of the room. Roll on CT4 +2.

data.DESCRIPTION: Rolled up to one side is a leather canvas. You lay it out and see a bloody image of Murataynie.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can re-hang this image and with a correct offering please the god, gaining 2 FP.

data.DESCRIPTION: Above you a large slab seems to quiver with any movement below. It is precariously balanced.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you move through, roll a D6 and add your Discipline. On a 7 or below it tumbles. Lose 10 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: Stones have been stacked on an old statue of Grakada in the corner here. You pull some aside.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can reinvigorate this old statue with a correct offering and please the god, gaining 2 FP.

data.DESCRIPTION: Running along the wall here are stone masks that seem to show a humanoid lizard creature's face.
data.ENCOUNTER: You cannot remove the masks, but they seem to stare into your soul. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is littered with chipped stone and a large column has fallen in its centre.
data.ENCOUNTER: There is a fountain in the corner which trickles into a stone bowl. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: The walls here appear to have frost on them. There is a magical source of cool air funnelling in.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you do not have anything to wrap yourself in lose 4 HP and then roll on L8TR.

data.DESCRIPTION: An ice bridge spans a pit here. It is not deep but is lined with short spikes. You must cross to proceed.
data.ENCOUNTER: Roll 2D6 and add your Shift. 10 or below, you slip and your foot dangles catching a spike. Lose 6 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: Dripping water has formed icicles making it feel cave like. A frozen warrior stands in the centre.
data.ENCOUNTER: It comes alive. Use TERRA MAGNA stats. If you survive, you spot 2 bags roll on BT2 +2 twice.

data.DESCRIPTION: Somehow it is snowing in this room, which forms large mounds. From one bursts an INGHETY.
data.ENCOUNTER: The beast charges you. You must fight it. If you survive, you find 2 bodies. Roll on BST2 +1 twice.

data.DESCRIPTION: Carved into the stone here is the relief of a Cyclops. Set into the beady eye is a HQ Emerald.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you have a knife or sharp piece of metal you can prise it free. If you do a patrol comes. Roll on L8P.

data.DESCRIPTION: Behind some broken planks you find a seam of coal has been carved into an image of Inhuneric.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can reinvigorate this sculpture with a correct offering and please the god, gaining 2 FP.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room feels more humid that the rest of the dungeon. There is some green slime on the walls.
data.ENCOUNTER: It also covers the floor. Roll a D6 and add your Precision. On a 7 or lower you slip over. Lose 4 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are numerous columns here carved into seaweed forms. The light shimmers green.
data.ENCOUNTER: You see something darting around and feel some movement. Nothing feels right. Roll on L8TR.

data.DESCRIPTION: The walls here are carved with rough glyphs that look like frogs and lizards. They are embossed.
data.ENCOUNTER: The air is ripe with the smell of swamp gas. If you have a cloth mask lose 2HP. Otherwise lose 8 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a shallow pool here and rising from it is the LIZARD KING. It snarls and attacks.
data.ENCOUNTER: You must fight the beast. If you survive, you search around and find a small chest. Roll on CT3 +3.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is lined with good stone and despite being dark and grimy you can tell it was refined.
data.ENCOUNTER: It is featureless, but when you look around your eye see the painting of a beast. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: Beneath some dirt, in a corner is a bone puppet of Maduva. There is a peg to hang it from.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can reinvigorate this toy with a correct offering and please the god, gaining 2 FP.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is a jumble of dusty items all broken and useless, including planks, statues and bricks.
data.ENCOUNTER: You carefully move through the space but as you do so you hear something trigger. Roll on L8TR.

L7SR.title: L7SR - LEVEL 7 SMALL ROOMS (6 SQUARES OR SMALLER)
L7SR.notes: Roll 2D6 for this table. If you roll a unique room that already exists on this level then re-roll. All exits are archways.
L7SR.flavorText: This space is too small to be a normal room but it is not a corridor. All exits are archways.

data.DESCRIPTION: As you enter a white and ginger cat runs off. However, it's too quick to catch. In a bowl on the floor is some cooked chicken.
Set into the wall here are a spade and pitchfork. They are held in place by a leather strap and bone toggle.
There are a range of rough paintings on the wall here. All show beasts and creatures on a broad plane. There is nothing more here.
Sitting on a low stool is a RED GUARD. They jump up and attack. If you survive, you find a bottle of water next to the stool.
There is a large mound of animal dung here that has been shoved into a corner. It stinks. There is nothing else here.
There is a small wooden box here. You can smash it open but lose 1 random FP. Inside you find two items. Roll on WIT1 and WIT2.
A low iron burner stands on its own. Inside the wood has burnt down, but is still warm. There are some glowing embers.
A dark hole set into the wall here serves as a hiding spot for an ASCUNDE who jumps out and attacks as you approach.
There is a scattering of dried animal faeces here and what appears to be some kind of gloopy liquid. This space smells like animals.
One of the walls here is undulating with Metamorphic magic. If sensed it rushes through you as you approach. Gain 4 HP. One time use.
Hanging from above is a swaying chain. It lashes at you, as if magically enchanted. Roll a D6 whenever you come through here. 3 or below, it hits and you lose 2 HP.

L8SR.title: L8SR - LEVEL 8 SMALL ROOMS (6 SQUARES OR SMALLER)
L8SR.notes: Roll 2D6 for this table. If you roll a unique room that already exists on this level then re-roll. All exits are archways.
L8SR.flavorText: This space is too small to be a normal room but it is not a corridor. All exits are archways.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are some fallen blocks of stone here.
There is a stone ledge here and on it are some scattered shards of pottery.
There is a large hand drawn symbol of a Maze on the wall here. The room is a sketch of an ominous looking Minotaur.
There is a torch sat in a sconce, attached to the wall here. It sheds a flickering orange light across the small space.
Someone has scrawled an arrow on the wall here. It points to the left.
This room is empty and simply a connecting space with stone lined walls.
The usual grey walls here have been replaced with a red stone which gives off a mild warming heat.
There is a blue and white tiled stone basin in the wall here. It is full of clear water.
There is a small hole in the wall here and as you look in you see a cave and a flurry of bats burst free to escape back along the passage.
There is a large stone mounted on the wall here. Scrawled on it is some alien writing that you do not understand.
There is a stone arch spanning this space. It seems more decorative than a supporting structure.

L10ST.title: L10ST - SPECIAL TREASURE TABLE
L10ST.notes: Roll 2D6 for this table. Make a note of the items on your Character Sheet.
L10ST.flavorText: You search the body and find...

data.DESCRIPTION: Wrapped in silk is a wondrous Item, the Gauntlets of Power. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
In a small sack are the Bracers of Blessing. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Wrapped in a piece of paper is an Amulet of Fire Resistance. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Rolled in some cloth is a Potion of Decisive Actions. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
In a bone box is a Potion of Regeneration. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Wrapped in square of hemp are 4 Explosive Marbles in a pouch. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Carefully wrapped in some cotton is Potion of Divine Shield. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Tucked in a leather case are 5 Throwing Daggers. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Tucked in a leather pouch is a Potion of Rancid Breath. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Inside a sandle is a Potion of Light. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.
Tucked in a leather case is a Wand of Lighting. Also, there is a pouch containing 200 GC.

L9LR.title: L9LR - LEVEL 9 LARGE ROOMS (32 SQUARES OR LARGER)
L9LR.notes: Roll 2D6 for this table. If you roll a unique room that already exists on this level then re-roll.
L9LR.flavorText: This space is larger than the average room being 32 squares or larger...

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is lined with single beds and hammocks. It is long abandoned. Dust covers everything. A ZOMBIE and a SKELETON rise up and lope towards you. You must fight.
There are many wet weeds hanging from the ceiling here. Beneath is the moist mire and slime is a HAXA. She sees you and screams in anger. You must fight.
There is an old stone tomb here. Upon it sits a DEFECTUS. They grab for their blade and attack, anger filling their eyes. You must fight. If you survive, roll on SLT2.
Mounted on a pedestal is a large stuffed EARTH CROC. As you approach, it suddenly flexes its body and attacks. You must fight it. As it is now an undead, it is at -2 to all stats.
This space has been cleaned out a long time ago. There are traces of blood but mainly only dust remains. There is something here, though. Roll on IAUT5.
A large statue of a robed figure stands in the centre. It has been smashed. On closer inspection you see that inside the torso of the figure is a NIGHT UMBRA. You must fight.
There is a sectioned off stone chamber in this space. It is securely locked (lock +4). If you crack the lock then you find a chest. Roll on CT4.
This space is filled with a thick smog and there are spikes in the floor. Pass a Precision Check PC8(2) or catch a spike. Lose D6 HP. Roll on IAUT4.
This space is filled with tall dark pillars. From the darkness a NIGHT UMBRA emerges and attacks. If you survive you find a chest. Roll on CT4.
The walls here are lined with cracks. In the corner is a SHUDDER FASA. They rise, visibly shaking and attack. If you survive there is a bag here. Roll on BT2.
Many beehives stand in this room. Roll a D6 and add your Discipline. On a 6 or lower the bees attack and you take 4 HP damage, otherwise you find some honey. Gain 2 Rations.

L9HA_Rooms.title: Level 9 Rooms - Human Ancestry - The Cursed
L9HA_Rooms.notes: Roll 2D6 (D66) for this table. If you roll a unique room that already exists on this level then re-roll. The recurring GREY MIST rooms (rolls 15, 25, 36, 44, 52, 66) all share a description; encounters vary.

data.DESCRIPTION: The stone here has been scarred and gouged, the long slashes deep and numerous in the dark stone.
data.ENCOUNTER: There are no objects of interest in this space. You feel a sense of foreboding though. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: Two stone pillars have toppled into this space and shattered. Upon the stone a red fungus has grown.
data.ENCOUNTER: If used in a smoke screen potion bomb the cloud does 2D6 damage. There is nothing else here.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is devoid of furniture. The walls seem to be covered in a nodules or boils. They throb.
data.ENCOUNTER: The space is foul and you quickly rush through, but are stopped by a patrol. Roll on L9P.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is full of scraps of wood and tumbled down stone. There is an area that is cleared.
data.ENCOUNTER: Sitting in the space is a NIGHT UMBRA. It slowly stands and spreads its wings. You must fight.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of a grey mist that blurs your vision. You get a sense that the room is empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: You feel around the walls for exits but you cannot explore the room itself. You continue onwards.

data.DESCRIPTION: The stones have been stripped back at one of the walls here, and inside the earth a statue forms.
data.ENCOUNTER: This is a sacred shrine to Grakada. If you can make 2 correct offerings, receive 2 FP and roll on L7BT.

data.DESCRIPTION: Bent over the rotting body of a huge bison are two skeletal figures. They jerk upwards as you enter.
data.ENCOUNTER: They lope towards you with the prospect of fresh meat. You must fight two MURIND.

data.DESCRIPTION: All the walls and floor here are charred and black. There was once a great fire in this space.
data.ENCOUNTER: There is some charcoal and ash here. The smell of burning wood is strong. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: Steam rises from boiling rocks and there is a trough of lava down the centre of the floor.
data.ENCOUNTER: Emerging from the steam is the RUBICUND. You must fight. If you survive, then roll on CT4 +2.

data.DESCRIPTION: In the centre of this room is a small stone fountain that bubbles blood into a narrow basin.
data.ENCOUNTER: This is a sacred shrine to Murataynie. If you can make 2 correct offerings, +2 FP and roll on L7BT.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of a grey mist that blurs your vision. You get a sense that the room is empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: You feel around the walls for exits but you cannot explore the room itself. You continue onwards.

data.DESCRIPTION: A mass of red fungus is creeping along the walls here. The room has been cleared of furniture.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can take some of the fungus. If you do roll a D6 and add your Discipline. 5 or lower lose 6 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: Three corpses hang from three central pillars. They are long dead. Their bodies are pox covered.
data.ENCOUNTER: These poor people are long dead, but as you enter you hear a noise. Roll on L8TR.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room appears empty at first but then you notice a concealed stone arch. You peer inside.
data.ENCOUNTER: You see an old DEFECTUS at a table, polishing a blade. They turn to you and stand. You must fight.

data.DESCRIPTION: The walls here are covered in black and blood red symbols that you cannot read.
data.ENCOUNTER: They have been scrawled with some ferocity. Beneath one are some piles of ash. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: When you enter this room thick tendrils lash down from the ceiling at you. You try to dodge them.
data.ENCOUNTER: Roll a D6. If you roll a double 4, you can use your Shift points and escape. Otherwise lose 10 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is covered in green glass tiles, a light behind some creating a green aura in the space.
data.ENCOUNTER: Sitting in the corner, examining a gemstone, is a JADE DIVINER. They turn to you. You must fight.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of a grey mist that blurs your vision. You get a sense that the room is empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: Suddenly out of the mist lurch two ZOMBIES. You must fight them before moving on.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a hole in the wall here. You cannot see inside as it is pitch black and swirling with energy.
data.ENCOUNTER: This is a sacred shrine to Intuneric. If you make 2 correct offerings, receive 2 FP and roll on L7BT.

data.DESCRIPTION: As you enter this barren space that has been scattered with sand and hay you see three figures.
data.ENCOUNTER: They turn to you and attack. Face three AFFLICTED. If you survive, you find some dried insects.

data.DESCRIPTION: A mass of branches has been heaped into one corner. They're covered with moss and twigs.
data.ENCOUNTER: There's movement beneath the hide and two ASCUNDE emerge. You must fight the pair.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of a grey mist that blurs your vision. You get a sense that the room is empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: You feel around the walls for exits but you cannot explore the room itself. You continue onwards.

data.DESCRIPTION: A circular pool is set into the floor here. It is full of swamp water and weeds. There is movement.
data.ENCOUNTER: The haggard form of a HAXA rises from the pool, dripping wet and draped in algae. They attack.

data.DESCRIPTION: Above, thousands of roots penetrate the ceiling. Tied into these are wooden idols and symbols.
data.ENCOUNTER: This is a sacred shrine to Radacina. If you make 2 correct offerings, receive 2 FP and roll on L7BT.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is lined with tables, on which are some wooden boxes. All are empty except the last one.
data.ENCOUNTER: Gas seeps out. Roll a D6 and add your Discipline. 5 or below you breathe it in. Lose 8 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of a grey mist that blurs your vision. You get a sense that the room is empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: You feel around the walls for exits but you cannot explore the room itself. You continue onwards.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of broken pots and items of furniture, as if the room has been ransacked.
data.ENCOUNTER: Standing to one side is a SHUDDER FASA. They turn to you, shaking uncontrollably and attack.

data.DESCRIPTION: Embedded, standing upright in the middle of the floor is a giant bone that is carved with symbols.
data.ENCOUNTER: This is a sacred shrine to Maduva. If you can make 2 correct offerings, receive 2 FP and roll on L7BT.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are two tables and some chairs here. On one table is a rusted knife. It is long abandoned.
data.ENCOUNTER: Roll a D6. 1-4= A patrol comes. Roll on L9P and you must fight. If you survive, roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: The walls here are covered in red scales. They radiate heat. There are some small piles of ash.
data.ENCOUNTER: This space is warm, you feel thirsty. You notice a log fire in the corner. If you use the fire lose 5 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: A swarm of insects drops down on you from the ceiling. The room is empty so there is no cover.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you wear plate, chainmail or scale armour you struggle to avoid them. Lose 5 HP. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a recess in the wall here. You look into it and your vision goes. You step back, it returns.
data.ENCOUNTER: This is a sacred shrine to Nevazator. If you make 2 correct offerings, receive 2 FP and roll on L7BT.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is an old man chained up here (lock +2). He whispers but you do not understand him.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you can free him, he touches your head and realise you have been blessed. Gain 8 HP. L1PR.

data.DESCRIPTION: You step into the room straight on to a pressure pad. A large slab swings toward you from a wall.
data.ENCOUNTER: Roll a D6 and add your Shift points. On a 7 or below you are slammed by a stone. Lose 10 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: You are shocked to see a cesspit. In it floats three bodies. The smell is horrid. Lose 2 HP as it is toxic.
data.ENCOUNTER: As you approach, the bodies rise as ZOMBIES. You must fight all three before moving on.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of a grey mist that blurs your vision. You get a sense that the room is empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: You feel around the walls for exits but you cannot explore the room itself. You continue onwards.

L10HA_Rooms.title: Level 10 Rooms - Human Ancestry - The Dungeon Lords
L10HA_Rooms.notes: Roll 2D6 (D66) for this table — 36 entries (1-1 through 6-6). PDF presents this as PART 1 (rolls 1-1 through 3-6) and PART 2 (rolls 4-1 through 6-6); merged here for consistency with other HA_Rooms tables. The Dungeon Lord sequence is: BLOOD POOL (3-6) → METAL CHAMBER (4-1) → SWAMP ROOM (4-2) → HIGH THRONE (4-3, FINAL). See tables_codex_rules.md for level 10 rules.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is full of a grey mist that blurs your vision. You get a sense that the room is empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: You feel around the walls for exits but you cannot explore the room itself. You continue onwards.

data.DESCRIPTION: The wall here is covered with a large mosaic. It shows a robed figure standing in a pool of blood.
data.ENCOUNTER: The stone is also red and the light crimson from one burning torch. There is nothing else here.

data.DESCRIPTION: One random exit doorway in this empty room has a magical barrier in it. Instead of what is listed.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you pass through this barrier, at any time, you lose 5 HP. Note it on the map with a star.

data.DESCRIPTION: The light seems to have been sucked from this space. You struggle to see into the dark corners.
data.ENCOUNTER: You pause and listen but you hear nothing. This space feels as if it is cursed. You move on.

data.DESCRIPTION: The stone panels around the walls show scenes of devastation. Mighty figures are pictured.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can pick out the legendary four lords as they lead armies across the surface, your home.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are a number of vases lined up on a shelf, each full of congealed blood as if recently filled.
data.ENCOUNTER: One is empty, splashes of blood on the floor. The smell here is grim and you quickly move on.

data.DESCRIPTION: In the middle here is a stone sarcophagus around which is an iron fence. It looks empty.
data.ENCOUNTER: On closer inspection you find the lid has fallen in. An impact from above caused it to shatter.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room appears to be a warning. Several human heads have been mounted on spikes.
data.ENCOUNTER: There is nothing else here, but as you go to leave you hear a noise. Roll on L8TR.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a large fur rug between two leather chairs here and a bookcase behind them.
data.ENCOUNTER: Nearby is a lit candle and you use it to inspect the books. You find a scroll tucked in. Roll on SCT3.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are piles of scrap metal in this space. All of it is too large to take, although there is wire.
data.ENCOUNTER: Some of it has been formed into a sculpture of a beast with curving horns.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are four metal lockers here (locks +3). There is nothing else in this space.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you can pick the locks you find 2 types of herbs in each one. For each locker roll twice on HST1.

data.DESCRIPTION: The walls here have been lined with fine stone and appear well maintained. There is a table here.
data.ENCOUNTER: Empty potion bottles and rolls of blank scrolls cover the table. There is also a black feather quill.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are shelves all around the wall here with, what look like, offerings spaced out on them.
data.ENCOUNTER: You examine them. Most are rotten foods. There are some mushrooms and Zoumerous leaves.

data.DESCRIPTION: Standing tall here is a statue of a red warrior. They wear a crown with two red spikes at the ears.
data.ENCOUNTER: There is a hatch in the base of the statue. Roll a D6. 1-3= It is empty 4-6= Roll on SECT2.

data.DESCRIPTION: The scene of a fight is laid out before you. The body of a warrior lays on its side in a pool of blood.
data.ENCOUNTER: The victor is nowhere to be seen. You search the body and find a potion in their pack. Roll on PT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: Floating about you is a massive orb of blue energy, of a magical origin which you have recognised.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can throw one item into this sphere and a duplicate will fall in the room. One time use.

data.DESCRIPTION: One random exit doorway in this empty room has a magical barrier in it. Instead of what is listed.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you pass through this barrier, at any time, you lose 5 HP. Note it on the map with a star.

data.DESCRIPTION: In the centre of this room is a large pool of blood. The walls also run with blood. The air is thick.
data.ENCOUNTER: As you enter the BLOOD LORD rises from the pool, dripping with blood. You must fight.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room has been lined with metal. Some of the panels glow red, the room is hot and oppressive.
data.ENCOUNTER: Standing in the centre, between two pillars is the FERRIC LORD. You must fight.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space is damp and covered in moss, fungi and sludge. Hair tangles hang from above.
data.ENCOUNTER: Pushing back the detritus the MIRE LORD appears. They attack. You must defend yourself.

data.DESCRIPTION: A tall, spired throne, made of black glass, occupies much of the far side of this refined room.
data.ENCOUNTER: The NETHER LORD rises, golden magic weaving shapes between their hands. You must fight!

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a large painting of a swamp that extends around the walls here. It is very unsettling.
data.ENCOUNTER: You feel faint. Roll a D6. Add your Discipline. 7 or lower, lose 5 HP. Keep rolling until you roll 8+.

data.DESCRIPTION: This space has been dressed with an elegant, creamy sandstone. The floor is finely laid.
data.ENCOUNTER: A tall candelabra stands in the centre of the room. All the candles are lit. Light flickers around the space.

data.DESCRIPTION: Numerous glass orbs hang above you out of reach. As you enter, they begin to fall and smash.
data.ENCOUNTER: Roll a D6 and add your Shift. 7 or below and you are caught by one. You lose 10 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: You have found a portal back to the town. You sense it's only temporary for it seems to shimmer.
data.ENCOUNTER: You can go back to town one additional time using the portal. Treat it as if using a ventilation shaft.

data.DESCRIPTION: Standing tall here is a statue of a muscular beast. They wear a crown with two curving horns.
data.ENCOUNTER: There is a hatch in the base of the statue. Roll a D6. 1-3= It's empty 4-6= Roll on SECT2.

data.DESCRIPTION: One random exit doorway in this empty room has a magical barrier in it. Instead of what is listed.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you pass through this barrier, at any time, you lose 5 HP. Note it on the map with a star.

data.DESCRIPTION: This empty room is lined with neat square stones. There is a range of black and red stones.
data.ENCOUNTER: You stand back and see these are forming huge wave patterns. A rising red tide.

data.DESCRIPTION: The stone in this room has been taken up and the floor is boggy and muddy, the air damp.
data.ENCOUNTER: Insects crawl through the mire and you see worms and slugs. A thick earthy smell fills the air.

data.DESCRIPTION: This room is lined with tall bookcases but they are all empty. There is a pile of paper in a corner.
data.ENCOUNTER: The writing on the parchments talks about mundane dungeon issues. Roll on IAUT4.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are wooden walls in this empty space. One is lined with spikes. As you enter it swings inwards.
data.ENCOUNTER: You jump aside. Roll a D6, add your Discipline. 8 or below and you are caught. Lose 8 HP.

data.DESCRIPTION: Hanging in groups across this space are wooden chimes carved with various runes. On the floor is a stick.
data.ENCOUNTER: If you play the chimes, gain 1 FP through magic resonance. But a guard comes. Roll on L10P.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a massive statue of a robed figure here carved in black slate. The edges are smooth.
data.ENCOUNTER: It appears as if this statue has been carved by magic. You place your hand on it. Gain 1 FP.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a wooden throne surrounded by 8 chairs here forming a circle. There is nobody here.
data.ENCOUNTER: You examine the throne. Roll a D6. 1-3= You find nothing. 4-6= You find a hatch. Roll on SECT2.

data.DESCRIPTION: There are a number of free standing walls here. On them are scrawled a range of runes.
data.ENCOUNTER: It appears as if someone has been experimenting with rune magic. There is a lump of charcoal here.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a sectioned off annex here. You peer inside and see five skeletons chained to the wall.
data.ENCOUNTER: The bodies look like they have been experimented on. There is a set of keys here and an old rag.

L9SR.title: L9SR - LEVEL 9 SMALL ROOMS (8 SQUARES OR SMALLER)
L9SR.notes: Roll 2D6 for this table. If you roll a unique room that already exists on this level then re-roll. All exits are archways. Note: L9 small rooms are 8 squares or smaller (vs 6 for L1-L8).
L9SR.flavorText: This space is too small to be a normal room but it is not a corridor. All exits are archways.

data.DESCRIPTION: The wall here is covered in poison pus. You can gather it, if added to Shrapnel Burst Potion Bombs it does an extra 5 damage. You lose 4 HP if you gather it.
Built into the wall here is a fountain with a trickle of water running into a stone basin. To your surprise the water is clear.
The torch on the wall here is lit and casts long shadows beyond the space.
There is an old corpse here that appears mummified. There is a pouch. If you take it Roll on PT3 but you kick up some toxic spore and lose 3D6 HP.
The good stone here has been hacked at with some large blade. Chips of stone litter the floor. In amongst it you find two broken claws.
A low iron burner stands in one corner. Inside it is cold and hasn't been used for a long time. There is some ash and charcoal inside.
There is a heap of stone and splintered wood here, pushed to one side. Otherwise, this dusty space is empty.
Embedded into a mud-packed wall are human teeth. You can prise a few free, but many are too impacted and cannot be removed.
There is a large piece of canvas paper nailed to the wall here. You cannot read the words for they are in a different language. The black ink is fresh.
On the floor here are the remains of about seven smashed potion bottles. Two though are still intact and contain clear water. They look like the bottles you have.
There is a dead crow hung up here as if a warning. Not much remains, in fact it is largely skeletal. However, you are able to salvage 2 black feathers.

L10SR.title: L10SR - LEVEL 10 SMALL ROOMS (8 SQUARES OR SMALLER)
L10SR.notes: Roll 2D6 for this table. If you roll a unique room that already exists on this level then re-roll. All exits are archways. Note: L10 small rooms are 8 squares or smaller (vs 6 for L1-L8).
L10SR.flavorText: This space is too small to be a normal room but it is not a corridor. All exits are archways.

data.DESCRIPTION: There is a golden idol worth +6D6 +6D6 GC here on a plinth. You can try and swap it out with a pouch of sand or sand. Make a precision check PC(9). Failure sees a dart shoot from a hole. Lose 4 HP.
There is a lantern here hanging from a hook. There is oil in the lamp font and it is removable, but a large flame. It can be lit and thrown for 2D6 damage at the start of a combat.
There is a metal box, with a coin slot, attached to the wall. The lock is old but strong (lock +2). If you manage to pick the lock, you find 3D6 GC and a MQ Gem.
On a pedestal here is a large book. It is too heavy to take but locked to the pages is a scroll. Roll a D6. 1-3= it is unreadable, 4-6= a magic scroll. Roll on SCT4.
There are some jars on a shelf here. In one are some beetles, and in another you find some herbs. Roll on HST1, twice. There used to be more but they have been taken.
A low iron burner stands on its own. Inside it is cold and hasn't been used for a long time. There is some ash and charcoal inside.
Painted on the wall here is a symbol that represents one of the Dungeon Lords. You feel it resonating a dark energy. You begin to choke and back away. Lose 5 HP.
Embedded into a mud-packed wall are animal fangs. You can prise a few free, but many are too impacted and cannot be removed.
There is a small wooden box here. You can smash it open but lose 1 random FP. Inside you find two items, roll on MIT1 and MIT2 and 2D6 +20 GC.
On the floor here are the remains of about seven smashed potion bottles. Two though are intact and contain cloudy water which is fine for making potions.
There is a floating orb here that seems to be full of swirling ink. If you touch it gain 12 HP. This can be used once and is considered a Minor Religious Artifact.

AIT.title: AIT - ADVENTURER INFECTED TABLE
AIT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you find yourself, due to the effect of an encounter, in the state of being infected. You can choose this from the table or roll 2D6 if more random. Table covers rolls 2-12 (11 entries).

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: Your vision has become hazy and you feel sick. Your skin has turned a grey colour. While you recover you lose 10 HP.
You feel a scratch on your neck. It begins to swell, you topple over and vomit. You must eat and rest. Lose 6 HP.
You feel unwell after the encounter. Lose a 1 HP per level of Adventurer.
You glance down at your arm where you were touched and see a green swelling you have to lance. Lose 4 HP.
You feel slightly hot after the encounter and begin to sweat. Rest up and eat something. Lose 2 HP and 1 ration.
You check for scratches. If you have some water, you clean up. Otherwise you do a rush job and lose 4 HP.
You look over your hands in case of scratches and find nothing.
You examine where you took a few knocks and find there is nothing to worry about.
Your body feels itchy after the encounter but this soon subsides with no ill effects.
It's not as bad as you thought. Gain 2 HP.
Strangely you feel invigorated after the encounter as if a god has blessed you for the deed. Gain 10 HP.

ANDT.title: ANDT - ADVENTURER NEAR DEATH TABLE
ANDT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you are on 1 or 2 HP only after combat. This table is very much about when an Adventurer is near death and gives you more of a fighting chance when applicable. Roll 2D6 for this table. Rows 7-12 are god-specific FP heals (each god grants 2 HP per FP spent, repeatable up to FP available).

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: There is nothing you can do, your wounds are serious. You are near death.
If you have some rags and sticks you can splint your wound and heal 1 HP.
You take a few moments to dab the wound, but you are very sore. Heal 1 HP.
You spend a bit of time tending to the wound and are able to stop the bleeding. Heal 2 HP.
You are able to hurriedly pull together enough clean fabric to patch up some of your wounds. Heal 4 HP.
Grakada looks upon you with pity. You may spend 1 Grakada FP to heal 2 HP. You can spend as many FP as you have.
Intuneric looks upon you with pity. You may spend 1 Intuneric FP to heal 2 HP. You can spend as many FP as you have.
Nevazator looks upon you with pity. You may spend 1 Nevazator FP to heal 2 HP. You can spend as many FP as you have.
Murataynie looks down on you. You may spend 1 Murataynie FP to heal 2 HP. You can spend as many FP as you have.
Maduva looks upon you with pity. You may spend 1 Maduva FP to heal 2 HP. You can spend as many FP as you have.
Radacina looks upon you with pity. You may spend 1 Radacina FP to heal 2 HP. You can spend as many FP as you have.

CIET.title: CIET - CAVE IN EVENT TABLE
CIET.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when there are any signs of damage to the room you just entered (this will be in the room description). Encounter anything in the room first and then roll for the cave in. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: You are stunned to sense shaking and see the walls collapse in. You take a pummelling. Lose 10 HP.
An ominous speech greets you as you explore the room. Rubble tumbles down on your back. Lose 2 large items and 4 HP.
You raise your arms as rocks tumble in. If you have a shield, it affords you some protection. Lose 2 HP. Otherwise lose 8 HP.
The ceiling is bowing. You see it topple and drop. Roll a D6 and add your shift. 8 or below you're caught, lose 6 HP.
One of the walls buckles pulling some of the ceiling down. Roll a D6 and add your shift. 7 or lower it catches you, lose 4 HP.
The ceiling here seems to shift and then collapses inwards. You jump back and avoid the rock and earth.
The ceiling above you moans and a thick timber snaps to burst through catching you slightly. Lose 2 HP.
You jump aside as the walls and ceiling collapse inwards trapping you. Lose 1 ration or 4 HP as you dig yourself free.
Dust bellows upwards and you rush out of the way as loose rubble falls to litter the floor. You find one exit is blocked.
The ceiling shifts and collapses, but it slowly crumbles and you're able to avoid it, sustaining no injury.
The wall topples over, pulling rubble down. Afterwards you find a concealed compartment in the wall. Roll on SECT1.

CIRT.title: CIRT - CAVE IN RESCUE TABLE
CIRT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you find a dead end room and there has been no creature encountered in the space. Encounter the room before rolling. Roll 2D6 for this table. Rows 10-12 reference L1PR / L2PR (presumed Player Rescue table — not yet captured in this codex).

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: Someone's trapped in the rubble and you go to help. As soon as you do they break free and attack. Roll on the level patrol table.
You approach slowly and see an enemy scrambling free. Roll on the level patrol table and defend yourself. They have -5 HP.
You spot an arm and shift a load of rocks, but it's a guard and with their last action they slice you with a knife. Lose 4 HP.
You quickly start moving stones when you spot a foot. The person beneath is near death. There is nothing you can do.
A supporting strut has toppled over, bringing stone down. You think you spot some clothing but it turns out to be nothing.
There is a massive mound of rubble here. You see a foot sticking out but when you check it is just a boot.
Half a wall has fallen in here. You are surprised to hear a noise coming from within but after a search you find nothing.
You uncover a person who is long dead. You search them and find some Ilios Petals and Kathoto Spines in a pouch.
There is a person trapped here. They are badly injured. If you have some bandages they survive and escape. L1PR.
There is a person trapped here. You shift some of the larger rocks and they break free and thank you. L1PR.
Rock and rubble has come down from the ceiling. Trapped beneath are 2 forms. You manage to free them. L2PR.

CLST.title: CLST - CLOSET SPACES TABLE
CLST.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when there is a 2x2 space on the map next to a room. This 2x2 space must be completely surrounded and considered a void on the map. Draw a 2x2 room adjoining the room — it is a closet large enough to walk into. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: The closet doors burst open. Roll on the patrol table for the level. They were waiting to surprise you and they attack first.
You open the doors to this empty space to a thwip. Roll a D6 and add your Shift. On a 6 or lower the dart hits you. Lose 4 HP.
You open the doors to a grim sight. A man hangs there and is long dead. There is nothing more in the space.
You open the doors and a dog burst out and escapes. You find in the room 2 mud caked potatoes.
Brushing back the dust you find the door. Inside on shelving is a collection of jars. In one are some pickled onions.
You open the double doors and find a dusty space full of cobwebs. There is nothing here.
You spot a door in the wall. Inside are some stacked boxes. All are empty apart from one, which holds a length of rope.
The space is cluttered. In amongst it you find 2 goblets worth 20 SC each and an empty potion bottle.
This is a storage space of some kind. On a shelf is some wrapped cheese. Gain 1 ration and a potion. Roll on POT2.
You force the closet doors open and are surprised to find a chest in the room. Roll on CT2.
You open the door and are surprised to see two people bound and gagged. You free them and they escape. L2PR.

CDPT.title: CDPT - CREATURE DRINKS A POTION
CDPT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when an enemy you are fighting has lost half their HP. They miss a round of combat as they drink the potion. Only humanoid enemies can drink potions. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: The combatant drinks a potion and breathes fire at you. You duck back but take 2D6 damage.
The combatant drinks a potion and shoots ice shards at you. You dodge but take D6 +3 damage.
Your enemy pulls out a yellow potion and quaffs it healing 20 HP.
Your enemy pulls out a green potion and quaffs it healing 10 HP.
Your foe draws a potion and drinks it. They become a blur and gain an extra attack.
Your enemy whips out a vial and drinks the blue liquid. It gains +1 to damage for this combat.
Your foe pulls out a potion and drinks it. They seem to glow. They take 2 less damage per hit for the rest of the combat.
Your enemy draws a potion, drinks and smashes it. You can tell magic will not affect them.
Your foe quaffs a clear potion and takes up a more balanced posture. They +1 Shift for the rest of the combat.
The combatant drinks a potion and chokes. It must have been poorly mixed. They lose 3 HP.
Your foe takes out a small vial, screams in triumph and swallows it down. They shudder and collapse dead.

CNDT.title: CNDT - CREATURE NEAR DEATH TABLE
CNDT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when a humanoid creature has been reduced to zero HP (not a regular death) and is dying. At this moment they may reveal a quest hook. Roll 2D6 for this table. Rows 5-10 are god-symbol quests (Grakada, Intuneric, Nevazator, Maduva, Murataynie, Radacina).

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: "The missing crown". They talk about a hatch in a statue. Add Hatch quest. Next time you find a hatch roll D6. 1-2= You find a golden crown worth 50 GC instead of the table result. If you fail you may try five more times.
"Karo betrayed me". They talk of a human who betrayed them. Add Karo quest. When you next fight a human, roll a D6. 1-2= it is Karo. They lose a Shift point as you have been told their name.

"Mind the trap". They seem repentant and talk about a stone trap. Add Stone Trap quest. If you encounter a trap that uses stone you recognise the layout as they described it and can avoid it with no consequences.

"I believe in Grakada". They hand you a symbol of the god as they die. Add Grakada's Symbol Quest. Next time you are able to make an offering to Grakada you offer up the symbol, receive 3 FP and complete the quest.

"I believe in Intuneric". They hand you a symbol of the god as they die. Add Intuneric's Symbol Quest. Next time you are able to make an offering to Intuneric you offer up the symbol, receive 3 FP and complete the quest.

"I believe in Nevazator". They hand you a symbol of the god as they die. Add Nevazator's Symbol Quest. Next time you are able to make an offering to Nevazator you offer up the symbol, receive 3 FP and complete the quest.

"I believe in Maduva". They hand you a symbol of the god as they die. Add Maduva's Symbol Quest. Next time you are able to make an offering to Maduva you offer up the symbol, receive 3 FP and complete the quest.

"I believe in Murataynie". They hand you a symbol of the god as they die. Add Murataynie's Symbol Quest. Next time you can make an offering to Murataynie you offer up the symbol, receive 3 FP and complete the quest.

"I believe in Radacina". They hand you a symbol of the god as they die. Add Radacina's Symbol Quest. Next time you are able to make an offering to Radacina you offer up the symbol, receive 3 FP and complete the quest.

"Add this to it". They seem repentant and pass you a strange herb. Add Extra Ingredient Quest. If you find a potion of healing you can add this ingredient to it and heal a further 15 HP to complete the quest.

"Please rescue Gren". They talk about a prisoner, give you a key. If you find a prisoner who is locked up roll a D6. 1-3= You have found Gren and can open the lock. You explain their captor repented. L1PR.

CRET.title: CRET - CREATURE RETREAT TABLE
CRET.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when the creature you are fighting is at less than 25% Health Points and it is their turn to hit. The enemy performs an action that may either help them or hinder them. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: The creature ducks, rolls and escapes your reach. As they do another creature of the same type enters the room. Fight both.
The creature dodges and backs out of reach. It grabs a vase and throws it at your head. Take D6 +4 damage.
The creature pulls back and you kill it with a jab but not before it slashes back. Take D6 +2 damage.
The creature releases a potion bomb. There is a burst of fire and you take D6 damage. The fight continues on.
The creature shows signs of wanting to escape but changes their mind and the fight continues as they attack again.
The creature tries to run but you shove it and gain an extra attack. The fight continues.
The creature is weaken, pulling away slightly. It misses its next attack.
The creature tries to pull back from the fight but you push forward and attack again. They miss a round.
The creature passes out from the pain allowing you to finish it off with one final blow.
The creature is terrified, flinching in pain, appearing panicked, allowing you to strike it dead.
The creature pulls away and stumbles backwards, falling as it does, allowing you to finish it off.

DEET.title: DEET - DEAD END EVENT TABLE
DEET.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you roll a dead end corridor that is five squares or longer and have the encounter at the end of the passage. It is a dead end and you do not always have to roll on this table. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: You reach the dead end and see why it was abandoned. The ceiling has fallen in and as you go to leave more tumbles down. Lose 10 HP.
At the end of the passage is a figure leaning against the wall. Roll on the level patrol table and face them.
There is a lantern hanging from the wall here. There are some picks leaning to one side. The tunnel is incomplete.
There are supporting beams here that have come down. You back away this area is clearly unsafe.
There is a small metal burner at the end of the corridor, with a stool beside it. The lit wood casts long shadows.
At first you think there is something there, some rubble obscuring your view, but on closer inspection it's nothing.
The end has been made into a cell. It is a drawn woman. The lock looks strong (+3 lock). If you can pick it you liberate 1 prisoner.
You notice the corridor slopes down and a pool of clear water has formed at the end. There are some fish in it. Gain 1 ration.
There is a body at the end. Some poor old adventurer has expired. You search his body and find some Scarlet Orievatus leaves.
The end of the passage has been made into a storage area. You search 3 crates and find some items. Roll on WIT1, MIT2 and ART2.
Some loot has been stashed at the end of the corridor. There is a tea chest and a chest. Roll on TCT1 and CT1.

DPQT.title: DPQT - DEAD PRISONER QUESTS TABLE
DPQT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you come across a dead prisoner either through rolling on a table or in the description of the room. There is a note on the body. Encounter anything in the room before rolling. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: The note mentions a magic user and a secret wand. Add Secret Wand quest. If you come across an enemy who uses magic, roll a D6. 1-2= You find a secret wand on the person. Roll on MWT1. Otherwise, you keep looking.

The note talks of a lost relative. There is a dagger with a crest. Add Crest quest. The next time you search a Sarcophagus roll a D6. 1-2= You find your lost relative and are able to return the dagger. Gain 2 FP of any God. Otherwise keep checking.

The note talks of lost treasure in a lost room. Add Lost Room quest. If you find a void on the map with no entrance roll a D6. 1-2= You find some rubble of a door. You open it and inside find a chest. Roll on CT2. Otherwise keep looking.

The note talks of a Herb Stash. Add Herb Stash quest. When you encounter a Hall of any type roll a D6. 1-2= You discover a loose stone and behind it a bag containing two herbs. Roll twice on HST1. Otherwise, you keep checking.
On the piece of paper is a diagram of a trap. Add Trap Diagram quest. When you encounter a trap that snares you can recognise the layout from the paper and pass it unscathed. The quest is completed.

On the note is a name drawn on a hooded figure. Add Robed Rune quest. If you face a robed figure in a combat, you crush the paper, drawing on the runes power and do an extra D6 damage on the first successful hit. It's then completed.

The note talks of some stolen jewellery. Add Jewellery Stash quest. When you come across a fountain roll a D6. 1-3= You find a hidden ledge underneath and on it a collection of jewellery worth 50 GC. Otherwise, you can keep checking.

The note talks of a hidden Ruby. Add Ruby Quest. Next time you defeat someone of the Infernal Order roll a D6. 1-2= You have found Hxater. He has a pendant hidden in his clothing. It is a HQ Ruby. Otherwise, keep checking.

The note shows some words translated into your language. Add Translation quest. The next time you find some text you cannot read you compare the words. Roll a D6. 1-3= Means you understand some of it. Gain 1 Discipline this level.

The note is attached to half a scroll. Add Half Scroll quest. If you face a magic user of level 4 or above and survive roll a D6. 1-3= They have the other half. You can combine them. Roll on SCT4 to find out what it is. Otherwise keep looking.

The parchment shows three runes. Add Three Rune quest. These are magical runes and if placed on an altar and blood is poured over them, they will enchant one piece of armour. You will become bloodied during and after the ritual. Roll on ENAT1.

FVT.title: FVT - FORCED VISION TABLE
FVT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you face a creature who appears to have some form of mental or psychic power. Use this via their attacks or as a description. Makes the fight harder. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: The creature forces a terrible vision of blood and death into your mind, that ripples through your body. Lose 10 HP.
The enemy forces an illusion of flashing lightning into your brain and you tumble back and lose 7 HP.
A vision of a crowd of people appears before you, confusing you. It runs from the enemy who strikes you for 5 HP damage.
Grey clouds surround your vision making it difficult to see and your head spins. Lose 2 HP and miss the next round.
The creature projects a vision into your brain that makes you see double. You do 5 points less damage with your next hit.
Blackness fills your vision as your enemy forces your mind into a dark place. You have -2 shift on your next attack.
The creature forces the image of a huge bear into your mind and you cower away. It fades and you miss the next round.
The creature tries to project the image of a spear flying at you but it fades before it hits you and you continue the fight.
The creature attempts to force an image of your death into your head but you look on with disbelief and continue the fight.
The foe tries to make itself invisible to your mind but you see through the mental camouflage and deliver the killing blow.
The enemy tries to inject your mind with sharp pain but you are able to rebound it back on them delivering the killing blow.

GINT.title: GINT - GOD INTERVENTION TABLE
GINT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you have taken the killing blow from an enemy or have failed your Recovery from Unconsciousness roll. This is a last ditch attempt to save your adventurer — the god reaches down to help or looks on with pity. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: As you stumble backwards, blood pouring from your wound, you look up and see the anger of Murataynie as a great beast appears and consumes you.
You collapse to your knees and look up to see the air full of Maduva's bones, all of which are beating out a death toll. They come tumbling down and crush you and your enemy.
The pain runs through your body as you feel death near. You look around, your foe before you and there is Intuneric, a swirling black mass who consumes you both.
For a second a divine power looks on but then there is nothing but death.
As the killing blow strikes you feel a surge of power and the damage you sustained from the blow is transferred to your enemy instead, saving you from death.
As you feel unconsciousness surround you see the glimmer of a flickering light. A divine power gifts you one more chance and you stand ready for another round. Gain 10 HP.
Blood smashes your face as the hit lands true. However, you do not die and instead see the vision of a robed figure before you touching your head. You stand again with 15 HP.
As you take the killing blow you welcome death. Instead, a god reaches down and restarts your heart. You have 15 HP and as you stand your foe is struck by an electric bolt and loses 10 HP.
You feel the steel rob you of your life and for a second coldness grips you, but from the ground roots rise up and animate you as Radacina bids you fight on with half your HP restored. +2 FP.
As you take the last blow, you feel unconsciousness overtake you and, in the darkness, you see Nevazator guiding you to the light. You wake, roll and rise with half your HP restored. +2 FP.
You fall on your side, the pain intense as the ground shifts and sucks you down. Within you hear Grakada's whisper and you are released to rise, rejuvenated with half your HP restored. +2 FP.

HCCT.title: HCCT - HIDDEN CHEST COMPARTMENT TABLE
HCCT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you loot a chest and it is empty. Perhaps the loot has been hidden in a secret compartment. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: You find the compartment and quickly open it. But a snake shoots out and bites your hand before slithering off. Lose 6 HP.
As you slide open the secret compartment a gas is released and you fall back inhaling most of it but lose 4 HP.
You smack your palm on the bottom of the chest and find it hollow. Alas, you flip the panel and it is empty.
You are able to prise open a panel and find some old but edible chunks of cheese, but not enough for a ration.
You pull up a fake bottom and inside find some cluttered objects including a potion bottle, some twine and a glass orb.
You tap the side and a panel flips open. Inside is a pouch containing some Dankomas Stems and Ilios petals.
You knock twice on the side of the chest and a door opens. Inside someone has stashed a pouch. Roll on PT2.
It is clear to see there is a panel at the bottom, you carefully open it. Inside is a bag. Roll on BT2.
It takes a while but you eventually manage to slide back a panel. In it wrapped in silk are 2 LQ gems. Roll twice on GMT1.
Concealed in the lid is a small compartment. You force it open and find inside a potion wrapped in cotton. Roll on POT2.
You flip up an ebony panel to reveal a velvet lined compartment. Resting on the soft material is a Staff of Claws.

INPT.title: INPT - INNATE POWER TABLE
INPT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when an enemy is losing the fight (bad rolls or a mighty injury). They pull their dignity together and call upon some innate power that is fully unexpected. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: The creature for a second writhes as it mutters something and suddenly a lightning bolt shoots from its hands. Lose 20 HP.
Heat prickles the air. Your enemy appears to glow as a fireball erupts from their palm. Lose 10 HP.
On the next successful hit the creature electrocutes you for an extra 5 damage.
The creature seems to shudder as armour plates appear across its skin. -2 extra damage from any hits on the foe.
A green glow surrounds the enemy and they gain 10 HP as they heal.
The creature summons up some hidden speed, lunges forward and gains an extra attack.
They pull back, hold a hand to their head and heal themselves for 15 HP. They miss a round of combat also.
Their arms appear double jointed. Next time they miss an attack roll they can roll again.
The creature's arms splay wide as a large tentacle rips open its chest killing them. It lashes you and falls limp. Lose 5 HP.
The creature seems to try and control a surge of power and in an instant is covered in ice, frozen to death.
Out of nowhere, as if possessed by a higher power, the creature rises off the ground and explodes.

MUCT.title: MUCT - MUSHROOM CONSUMPTION TABLE
MUCT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when you find a mushroom through searching a space or it is turned up as some loot. Not all mushrooms are the same and can have different effects, good or bad. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: The magic in the mushroom does not sit well. Madness fills your mind. You destroy all your potions before you recover.
The mushroom tastes foul and makes you feel sick instantly. It was a mistake. Lose 10 HP.
Eating the mushroom is pleasurable but soon after you feel sick and vomit. Lose 5 HP.
You consume the mushroom and feel dizzy. Next combat -1 shift. It wears off after.
It tastes nice but soon you regret eating it and feel sick. Lose 2 HP.
The mushroom seems to have no effect at all.
You chew on the rubbery mushroom and it releases a warming sensation. Gain 2 HP.
The mushroom is tasty and unexpectedly fills you up. You do not need to eat a ration when you go down to the next level.
Surprised you see the room grow bigger and feel elated. Energy pulses through you. Gain 20 HP.
You feel electricity charge through you once it is eaten. In the next combat do an extra 5 damage per successful hit.
You feel a deep knowledge wash over you. This was a sacred food. Roll on GOT1 and gain 2 FP for that god.

RMRT.title: RMRT - RARE MEDICAL REMEDIES TABLE
RMRT.notes: Optional table. A selection of rare medical remedies that can be added to the game if more options are wanted. Each has a description and ingredients to source. Pick one to attempt or roll a D6 for random selection. NOT a 2D6 outcome table — entries 1-6 are recipes, not random results.

data.NAME: Oil of Repair
data.EFFECT: Restores one damaged piece of armour to a new condition.
data.INGREDIENTS: 1) Water 2) Fat from an animal 3) Hemp 4) Thread 5) Potion of Finesse 6) Kathoto Spines 7) Dankoma Stems

data.NAME: Blinding Ointment
data.EFFECT: A remedy that is used against an enemy. When thrown in their eyes it blinds them for three rounds of a combat. The enemy is -3 Shift.
data.INGREDIENTS: 1) Crushed Beetles 2) Sand 3) Scarlet Orievatus Leaves 4) Kathoto Spines

data.NAME: Golden Elixir
data.EFFECT: Restores all your Health Points and 10 HP above the base line.
data.INGREDIENTS: 1) Water 2) Ilios Petals 3) Dankoma Stems 4) Zoumerous Leaves 5) Scarlet Orievatus 6) Kathoto Spines 7) Malako Leaves

data.NAME: Callus Doll
data.EFFECT: Anointed with the owner's blood, will absorb 20 damage or one fatal attack.
data.INGREDIENTS: 1) Cloth 2) Thead 3) Straw 4) Small sharp piece of metal 5) Your blood 6) Kathoto Spines 7) Zoumerous Leaves

data.NAME: Radiant Seaweed
data.EFFECT: You feel gills growing on your neck. If there is water in the room and you are wet you gain +2 Shift on your first turn of combat. You lose the gills when you become dry.
data.INGREDIENTS: 1) Water 2) A plant found growing in water 3) Potion of Finesse 4) Zoumerous Leaves

data.NAME: Paste of Slow Healing
data.EFFECT: Once applied it heals D6 HP per room for 3 rooms.
data.INGREDIENTS: 1) Animal Fat 2) Ilios Petals 3) Scarlet Orievatus 4) Malako Leaves

RAPT.title: RAPT - RARE POTIONS TABLE
RAPT.notes: Optional table. Use these potions as you like and when you wish. They are optional. Pick or roll a D6 for random selection. NOT a 2D6 outcome table — entries 1-6 are recipe/loot items.

data.NAME: Potion of Full Health
data.EFFECT: Restores all your Health Points.

data.NAME: Potion of Ethereal Visage
data.EFFECT: You become hard to hit as your body becomes partially translucent. The enemy -1 Shift Point for 1 whole combat.

data.NAME: Potion of Giant Size
data.EFFECT: You grow to double your height and cause double damage for one combat.

data.NAME: Potion of Astral Projection
data.EFFECT: You have an out of body experience and gain +1 to any searches in the next room. You can also open one locked door.

data.NAME: Potion of Whirlwind
data.EFFECT: You get 5 attacks per round for 1 whole combat.

data.NAME: Potion of Mirror Image
data.EFFECT: Projects 5 images of yourself for the combat. The enemy must roll a 1 on a D6, as a separate roll, as well as making a successful hit roll if they roll a 1. Lasts for 2 rounds.

RAST.title: RAST - RARE SCROLLS TABLE
RAST.notes: Optional table. Use these scrolls as you like and when you wish. They are optional. Pick or roll a D6 for random selection. NOT a 2D6 outcome table — entries 1-6 are recipe/loot items.

data.NAME: Scroll of Shrinking
data.EFFECT: Targets a limb on the enemy, shrinking it, causing 10 HP, nullifying any related interrupt.

data.NAME: Scroll of Thieving Trait
data.EFFECT: Steal a Manoeuvre from an enemy for one combat. You lose it once the combat is over. The foe cannot use it during combat.

data.NAME: Scroll of the Fake God
data.EFFECT: You take on the visage of a god and the enemy bows low allowing you to strike causing 3D6 damage.

data.NAME: Scroll of Sudden Blast
data.EFFECT: A burning wind does 15 HP damage to all enemies in the room. Lasts for 1 whole combat.

data.NAME: Scroll of Life Leach
data.EFFECT: Steal 10 HP from your enemy and add it to your own.

data.NAME: Scroll of Animate
data.EFFECT: If there is a statue in the room it comes to life and attacks the foe, swinging once before crumbling, causing 3D6 damage.

SERT.title: SERT - SECRET ROOMS TABLE
SERT.notes: Optional table. Can be applied when (like the Closet Spaces table) the space for the room must be completely surrounded and considered a void on the map that cannot be accessed. Generate the dead end room as normal, on a new exit for the room. Roll 2D6 for this table.

data.DESCRIPTION AND EFFECT: Secret Sanctum - As soon as you open the door you feel terrible heat wash over you. There are three INFERNAL MONKS here who run and attack.
Secret Resting Post - Resting on a stool by a burner is a humanoid. They stand when they see you. Roll on the level patrol. You must fight.
Secret Laboratory - As you enter you disturb two APOTHECARIES working at a table. You must fight them. If you survive, roll on POT2 and POT3.
Secret Crypt - There is a sarcophagus here. If you open it roll on L4UN and fight the undead. If you survive, roll on SLT2.
Secret Shrine - There is a small stone universal shrine here. It is a holy place and if you call the favour of the gods here you -1 from the roll.
Secret Pantry - Someone has taken the time to conceal a pantry with shelving. It is abandoned but on one shelf are some pickles. Gain 1 ration.
Secret Storage - There are some crates in this room. They are covered in dust. Roll on TCT1 twice.
Secret Quarters - You slowly open the door and find a simple bed and a cupboard. You search the space. Roll on BT2 and TAT1.
Secret Portal - There a churning portal here that can take you back to town once. You may return to town an additional time on this level.
Secret Library - The walls are lined with books and scrolls. You rummage through and find two unusual scrolls. Roll on SCT3 and SCT4.
Secret Vault - Standing on its own in the centre of this room is a large iron chest. It is locked (+4 lock). If you can pick the lock roll on CT4 +3.

LPT.title: Liberating Prisoners Table
LPT.notes: From Core Rules. Roll D6 once per liberated prisoner — references like 'L1PR' / 'L2PR' in other tables mean to roll on this table 1 or 2 times respectively. Roll values 3 and 4 share a single outcome.

data.DESCRIPTION: You hear nothing more about the prisoner.
The father of the prisoner approaches you outside the tavern. He hands over a pouch of 6D6 SC and thanks you.
You hear that the prisoner was a pious worshipper of a random god. Roll on GOT1 and gain a Favour Point for that god.
The prisoner finds you and gifts you some herbs. Roll on HST1.
The prisoner was from a rich family. A representative approaches you and gives you a pouch of 4D6 GC as thanks.

MBT.title: Market Bartering Table
MBT.notes: From Core Rules. Roll 2D6 with applicable bartering modifiers when selling loot in town.

data.DESCRIPTION: Taken to the cleaners. You lose 50% of the sell price of your loot (rounded up).
You come out thinking you did well, but when you add it up, you find you have lost 10% of the sell price (rounded up).
You manage to achieve the sell price of the loot.
You did well and stood strong on a couple of deals. You add 10% of the sell price of the loot (rounded up).
Masterful in the moment you convince the traders of the additional worth. Gain 20% of the sell price (rounded up).

HBT.title: Herbalist Table
HBT.notes: From Core Rules. Reference table — buy and sell prices for herbs at town herbalists. Prices are per 1 dose.

TET.title: Tavern Exploits Table
TET.notes: From Core Rules. Roll 2D6 for events while staying at the tavern between dungeon runs.

data.DESCRIPTION: Word around the tavern is that the dungeons are infested. When you encounter any small rooms (six squares or fewer) on the same level where you re-enter the dungeon, you also encounter a patrol.
The ale is flowing and you drink a little too much. You wake up with a hangover. Lose all SC and 2 HP before you re-enter the dungeon.

The tavern is very quiet this evening, but it does allow you to talk to Freda tending bar. She talks about some of the characters who have been in lately, including one group talking about finding another entrance to the dungeon and a distinctive stone that marks the spot. When you enter the dungeon again, you may exit that level one additional time (rather than pushing on to the next level before you can find another exit shaft).
You have a pleasant evening but nothing useful is discussed or wisdom shared.

You catch up with old Glen. He laughs as he talks about his exploits in the underworld. You listen intently to some of the same old stories. He talks about one particular stone that seems familiar. You may select the room type for the first new room you encounter when you re-enter the dungeon.
You're lucky, Freda has made some wholesome broth. You devour a large bowl of it and feel great when you wake the next day. Gain 2 temporary HP above your baseline.

You listen to the talk in the tavern and the mood is buoyant. People say there have been no raids in a couple of weeks, and that the enemy is low in numbers. On the level where you re-enter the dungeon, there are no patrols. If instructed to roll for one, ignore it.

AWT.title: Arm Wrestle Table
AWT.notes: From Core Rules. Roll 2D6 with applicable modifiers for arm wrestling at the tavern.

data.DESCRIPTION: The crowd around your table laughs at your defeat as your arm is slammed down by a large brute with bulging muscles. It seems to have raised your shoulder. Lose 2 HP, and 5 GC (or equivalent) if you have it.

You're so preoccupied with trying to beat a lean woman with thick weights that you don't notice a thief cut your pouch free and steal 10 GC (or equivalent coins) if you have it. You lose the round when you realise your belt suddenly feels lighter.
You manage to win a few rounds before a broad veteran rolls up and beats you all too quickly. He smiles, flips you 1 GC and says, "Next time kid."

It is a good night. You beat three tough-looking sailors and call it quits, deciding you have done well and shouldn't push your luck. You feel strong and confident that your quest in the dungeon will go well tomorrow. Gain 4 temporary HP above your baseline.

You are doing well and the crowd cheers you on, but you have pulled a muscle. During the break, a man comes up to you and gives you a healing salve you rub onto your forearm. He has backed you to win and wants to see a return on his investment. Alas, you fail in the final but still feel the effects of the salve. Gain 8 HP above your baseline.

You progress deep into the arm wrestling tournament and smash the final with a fine victory. You stand upon the table, triumphant, fellow drunk revellers cheering your name. You have proven to yourself that you have a keen focus. Gain 1 Discipline permanently. You can only do this once in the Arm Wrestling competition. If it happens again, gain 2D6 GC.

YLR.notes: From Core Rules. D6 list of legendary artefacts awarded at game's end (e.g., for defeating the Nether Lord).

The Helm of Zebor

The Wand of Dretas

HRT.title: Herbal Remedies Table
HRT.notes: From Core Rules. Reference table — recipes for crafting herbal remedies from harvested ingredients.

data.RECIPE_AND_EFFECT: Crushed Ilios Petals and Malako Leaves – applied to wound. Heals 10 HP. Can be used once per room.
Cut Dankoma Stems and Scarlet Orievatus – added to bandage and applied to wound. Requires cloth. Heals 20 HP. Can be used once per room.
Crushed Scarlet Orievatus, Ilios Petals, and Zoumerous Leaves. Requires a solid container. Makes 3 doses, no need to roll. Each heals 10 HP per dose. Can be used once per room.
Squeezed Kathoto Spines and crushed Zoumerous Leaves. Requires a container that can hold liquid. Heals all fresh damage caused by fire in the last two rooms you were in.
Crushed Dankoma Stems and Ilios Petals – creates a light powder that absorbs water quickly. Requires a container. Removes Soaked status.
Crushed Dankoma Stems and Scarlet Orievatus – creates a dense powder that cleans off blood. Requires a container. Removes Bloodied status.
